title: Lathyrus elongatus
intro: Lathyrus elongatus, also known as the Italian vetch or slender pea, is a perennial legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus elongatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and as a natural remedy for various inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Lathyrus elongatus is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It belongs to the genus Lathyrus and is classified as a perennial or annual herbaceous plant. The classification of Lathyrus elongatus is further divided into the subfamily Faboideae, tribe Loteae, and the genus Lathyrus itself. This species is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and parts of Europe. The exact classification of Lathyrus elongatus may vary depending on the source and the specific taxonomic classification system used.
uses: Lathyrus elongatus, also known as the purple pea or purple vetchling, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its seeds and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant's seeds are thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Lathyrus elongatus.

side_effects: Lathyrus elongatus is known to cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness, spasms, and even paralysis, if consumed in large quantities over an extended period. This condition occurs due to the presence of a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid) in the plant. Neurolathyrism can lead to permanent damage to the nervous system and is often irreversible. Consuming Lathyrus elongatus in moderation is essential to avoid these severe side effects. The risk of neurolathyrism can be minimized by avoiding the plant's seeds and only consuming the leaves and flowers.
